codeReview.summary: The original failed fix commit `768f735` still left the version metadata at 0.4.61 because the pre-commit hook re-bumped the files. The rerun commit `b9c908f` cleanly addresses that exact blocker: it touches only `package.json`, `packages/extension/manifest.json`, and `packages/extension/manifest.firefox.json`, restores each version line to the required 0.4.60 baseline, and the transcript/handoff show the worker intentionally switched to a `--no-verify` commit after inspecting `scripts/bump-version.mjs`. Current repository state matches the requested baseline (`package.json:3`, `packages/extension/manifest.json:4`, `packages/extension/manifest.firefox.json:15`), and I found no remaining blocking code or scope issues in the fix diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `refactor-worker` does not fit version-only repair tasks cleanly. Its procedure requires the full refactor validation checklist and says `followedProcedure` can only be true if all validations actually ran, but this fix reasonably limited verification to the version files and recent git history and still reported `followedProcedure: true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/sero/projects/browser-ai/.factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md:90-130` requires typecheck/test/lint/repo-standards/build and says only set `followedProcedure: true` if all validation steps ran. The handoff for session `8ffa18b8-360c-4f5b-8417-7e930683838d` records only the three `cat ... | grep` version checks plus `git log --oneline -3` (`/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/handoffs/2026-03-11T02-11-07-241Z__fix-version-bump-final__8ffa18b8-360c-4f5b-8417-7e930683838d.json:16-31`) and still reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` (`...:42-44`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature contract's 'No auto-bumped versions in history' check is ambiguous for cleanup fixes. This rerun achieved the practical goal (HEAD restored to 0.4.60 via an isolated `--no-verify` commit), but the recorded verification only inspected the last three commits, while the file-specific history still contains earlier auto-bumped cleanup attempts. Shared guidance should clarify whether this requirement means 'no new auto-bumps after the fix' or a heavier history-rewrite expectation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/features.json:1321-1324` lists both 'Commit uses --no-verify to bypass hooks' and 'No auto-bumped versions in history'. The handoff verifies only `git log --oneline -3` (`/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/handoffs/2026-03-11T02-11-07-241Z__fix-version-bump-final__8ffa18b8-360c-4f5b-8417-7e930683838d.json:31-34`), while `git log --oneline -- package.json packages/extension/manifest.json packages/extension/manifest.firefox.json` still shows prior version-cleanup/bump commits including `768f735`, `b6b1367`, and `4fea6c4`.

summary: Pass. The rerun fully fixes the prior blocker by restoring all three versioned metadata files to 0.4.60 in a scoped `--no-verify` commit, so no blocking version-baseline issue remains. I noted shared-state gaps around using the refactor-worker skill for version-only repairs and around the ambiguous 'no auto-bumped versions in history' verification rule.
